maml:description.maml:para: Compares two images and optionally saves a difference mask.
When OutputPath is omitted, the cmdlet writes the comparison result to the pipeline instead of creating a file.
When provided, the cmdlet writes a visual difference image instead of only returning the comparison result.
Converts an image to a different format.
Outputs a new file using the extension from OutputPath.
When OutputPath ends with .ico, the file is only copied if the source file is also an icon.
The extension determines the output format.
Quality for JPEG or WEBP images.
Returns a SixLabors.ImageSharp.Image object for further processing.
Merges two images and saves the result.
Resize images to fit before merging.
Possible values: Bottom, Right, Top, Left
Supported formats depend on the file extension.
Used with Height when not using .
Used with Width when not using .
Applies uniform scaling relative to the original size.
Only valid when resizing by width or height.
Saves an image to disk or returns its encoded bytes as a stream.
Use this cmdlet to persist an Image instance after applying transformations.
When omitted, the image is saved using the path already associated with the image object.
Quality for JPEG or WEBP images.
When used without FilePath, the cmdlet writes a stream object to the pipeline.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Save as JPEG with quality 80 ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Applies optional brightness, contrast, lightness, opacity, saturation or sepia adjustments.
Supported formats depend on the file extension.
Destination path when saving to disk.
Stream that receives the avatar image.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Generate an avatar and open it ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Reads barcode information from an image file.
Possible values: Code128, Code93, Code39, KixCode, UPCE, UPCA, EAN, DataMatrix, PDF417
Output path for the barcode image.
Converts a Base64 encoded string into an image file.
Path where the image will be written.
Open the newly created file after saving.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Save the Base64 content to a PNG file ----------
---------- Example 2: Decode and open the image ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Converts an image file into a Base64 encoded string.
Path to the image to convert.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Get a Base64 string from an image ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Applies a Gaussian blur with the specified Amount.
Supported formats depend on the file extension.
Specifies the strength of the blur.
Creates an image chart from definitions.
Use this cmdlet to render one or more chart definitions into a final image file.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a simple bar chart and save it ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Builds chart definitions inside a script block and renders them into a PNG file.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a line chart with annotations and preview it ----------
command:example.dev:code: $defs = @( New-ImageChartLine -Name 'CPU' -Value 35,42,58,61,49 -Color LimeGreen -Smooth ) $ann = @( New-ImageChartAnnotation -X 3 -Y 61 -Text 'Peak' -Arrow ) New-ImageChart -Definition $defs -Annotation $ann -FilePath cpu-usage.png -Theme Dark -ShowGrid -Show

dev:remarks.maml:para: Renders a themed line chart and overlays an annotation highlighting the peak value.

maml:description.maml:para: Use this cmdlet with New-ImageChart to highlight notable points on a generated chart.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates an annotation definition that can be passed to New-ImageChart via -Annotation or -AnnotationsDefinition.

maml:description.maml:para: Use this cmdlet inside New-ImageChart to define one bar series.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a single bar-series definition that can be passed into New-ImageChart.

maml:description.maml:para: Use this cmdlet inside New-ImageChart to define a line-series dataset.
Shape of markers placed on data points.

Possible values: None, FilledCircle, OpenCircle, FilledSquare, OpenSquare, FilledTriangleUp, OpenTriangleUp, FilledTriangleDown, OpenTriangleDown, FilledDiamond, OpenDiamond, Eks, Cross, VerticalBar, HorizontalBar, TriUp, TriDown, Asterisk, HashTag, OpenCircleWithDot, OpenCircleWithCross, OpenCircleWithEks, CircleWithLineLeft, CircleWithLineRight, TriangleWithLineLeft, TriangleWithLineRight
Render the line using a smooth curve.

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a smoothed line-series definition ready to be rendered by New-ImageChart.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates radial gauge chart data item.
Creates a cropped version of an image using rectangular, circular or polygonal areas.
Path to the image being cropped.
Where to save the cropped image.
X coordinate of the circle center.
Y coordinate of the circle center.
Open the cropped file after creation.
Gets EXIF metadata from an image.
Return dictionary with tag names and values.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Return translated EXIF as properties ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Removes EXIF metadata from an image.
Sets an EXIF tag value in an image.
The value must match the type declared by the selected EXIF tag, including ImageSharp wrapper types such as Number or Rational.
When not specified the source file is overwritten.
Creates an animated GIF from existing images.
Source image paths used as frames.
Output path for the GIF animation.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Build a GIF from two images ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Creates an icon file from an image.
Generates multiple icon sizes using Size.
Defaults to common icon sizes when empty.
Optional path to write metadata JSON.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Save metadata to a file ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Destination image path. Defaults to FilePath.
Creates a mosaic image from multiple files.
Number of columns in the mosaic.
Reads QR code information from an image file.
Returns the decoded content and symbology details.
Generates a QR code image from plain text content.
Use this cmdlet when the QR payload is already available as a raw string.
Content encoded in the QR code.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Create the QR code with a transparent background.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Show QR code after generation ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a BezahlCode QR for German payments.
Use this cmdlet to render BezahlCode payment payloads for German banking scenarios.
Possible values: SinglePayment, SinglePaymentSepa, SingleDirectDebit, SingleDirectDebitSepa, PeriodicSinglePayment, PeriodicSinglePaymentSepa, Contact, ContactV2
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic BezahlCode payment QR ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a standard payment QR code that can be scanned by BezahlCode-aware banking apps.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a branded BezahlCode image ----------
command:example.dev:code: New-ImageQRCodeBezahlCode -Authority SinglePayment -Name 'Evotec GmbH' -Account '1234567890' -Bnc '10020030' -Iban 'DE12500105170648489890' -Bic 'COBADEFFXXX' -Reason 'Consulting Retainer' -FilePath bezahl-brand.png -ForegroundColor Navy -BackgroundColor WhiteSmoke -PixelSize 16 -Show

dev:remarks.maml:para: Produces a payment QR code with custom styling and opens it after generation.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code for Bitcoin-like payments.
Use this cmdlet to create payment request QR codes for Bitcoin and similar supported cryptocurrencies.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic Bitcoin payment QR ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a payment QR code that includes the destination address and requested amount.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a crypto donation QR code with label and note ----------
command:example.dev:code: New-ImageQRCodeBitcoin -Currency Bitcoin -Address '1BoatSLRHtKNngkdXEeobR76b53LETtpyT' -Amount 0.005 -Label 'Evotec Donation' -Message 'Thank you for supporting the project' -FilePath donation.png -ForegroundColor DarkOrange -PixelSize 18 -Show

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a donation-style payment QR code and opens it after creation.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a calendar event QR code image.
Use this cmdlet to encode meeting or appointment details into a QR code that can be scanned into calendar applications.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Specifies whether the event spans the full day.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a timed meeting QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a QR code for a calendar event with explicit start and end times.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create an all-day calendar event QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates an all-day event payload and opens the QR image after creation.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code that opens an email draft.
Use this cmdlet to create scannable mailto-style QR codes for support, sales, or campaign responses.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic email QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a QR code that opens the default mail client with recipient, subject, and body prefilled.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a support-contact QR code ----------
command:example.dev:code: New-ImageQRCodeEmail -Email 'support@evotec.pl' -Subject 'Support Request' -Message 'Please describe the issue before sending.' -FilePath support-mail.png -ForegroundColor DarkSlateBlue -PixelSize 14 -Show

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a support-oriented email QR code and opens the image after creation.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code with geolocation data.
Use this cmdlet to create location QR codes that open map applications at a specific coordinate.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic geolocation QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a QR code that opens the target coordinates in compatible map applications.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create an event-location QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a location QR for signage, invitations, or venue directions and previews it immediately.

maml:description.maml:para: Use this cmdlet to create SEPA payment QR codes for European bank transfers.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic Girocode payment QR ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a payment QR code with the core SEPA transfer fields.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a Girocode for invoice payment ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a branded invoice-payment QR code and opens it after creation.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code for a Monero transaction.
Path to save the QR code image.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.
Generates a QR code for one-time-password configuration.
Use this cmdlet to create QR codes for authenticator apps that support TOTP or HOTP provisioning.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a TOTP QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a QR code that can be scanned into a typical authenticator app for time-based MFA.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create an HOTP QR code with a custom counter ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a counter-based OTP payload for systems that use event-driven one-time passwords.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code for dialling a phone number.
Use this cmdlet when you want a scan action to immediately open the dialer with a predefined number.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic phone-number QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a QR code that opens the dialer with the selected number.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a support hotline QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a styled call-now QR code suitable for posters, intranet pages, or support desks.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code for a Shadowsocks configuration.
Use this cmdlet to share a client-ready Shadowsocks connection string as a scannable QR code.

Possible values: Chacha20IetfPoly1305, Aes128Gcm, Aes192Gcm, Aes256Gcm, XChacha20IetfPoly1305, Aes128Cfb, Aes192Cfb, Aes256Cfb, Aes128Ctr, Aes192Ctr, Aes256Ctr, Camellia128Cfb, Camellia192Cfb, Camellia256Cfb, Chacha20Ietf, Aes256Cb, Aes128Ofb, Aes192Ofb, Aes256Ofb, Aes128Cfb1, Aes192Cfb1, Aes256Cfb1, Aes128Cfb8, Aes192Cfb8, Aes256Cfb8, Chacha20, BfCfb, Rc4Md5, Salsa20, DesCfb, IdeaCfb, Rc2Cfb, Cast5Cfb, Salsa20Ctr, Rc4, SeedCfb, Table
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic Shadowsocks QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a QR code for importing a Shadowsocks connection into a compatible client.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a tagged Shadowsocks client profile QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a named client profile QR code and opens it immediately after creation.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code initiating a Skype call.
Use this cmdlet for legacy Skype calling scenarios where a QR scan should start a call with a specific account.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic Skype-call QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a QR code that opens Skype and targets the selected username for a call.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a helpdesk Skype QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a branded Skype call QR code and opens the resulting image after creation.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a Slovenian UPN QR payment code.
Use this cmdlet when a prepared Slovenian UPN payment payload should be rendered into a scannable QR image.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a Slovenian UPN QR payment code ----------
command:example.dev:code: $upn = [CodeGlyphX.Payloads.SlovenianUpnQrPayload]::new('John Doe','Main Street 1','Ljubljana','Evotec d.o.o.','Business Street 2','Maribor','SI56192001234567890','Invoice 2026-041',19999) New-ImageQRCodeSlovenianUpnQr -Payload $upn -FilePath upn.png

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a UPN payment QR code from a complete Slovenian payment payload object.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a branded UPN QR code and preview it ----------
command:example.dev:code: $upn = [CodeGlyphX.Payloads.SlovenianUpnQrPayload]::new('John Doe','Main Street 1','Ljubljana','Evotec d.o.o.','Business Street 2','Maribor','SI56192001234567890','Annual subscription',4900) New-ImageQRCodeSlovenianUpnQr -Payload $upn -FilePath upn-brand.png -ForegroundColor DarkGreen -PixelSize 14 -Show

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a styled payment QR code and opens the resulting image after generation.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a QR code containing an SMS message.
Use this cmdlet when a scan should open the SMS app with recipient and optional message body prefilled.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a basic SMS QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a QR code that opens the SMS app with the recipient number and message body prefilled.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create an RSVP SMS QR code ----------
command:example.dev:code: New-ImageQRCodeSms -Number '+48 500 600 700' -Message 'RSVP: I will attend' -FilePath rsvp-sms.png -ForegroundColor Teal -PixelSize 16 -Show

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a ready-to-send RSVP QR code for invitations or registration desks.

maml:description.maml:para: Generates a Swiss QR payment code.
Use this cmdlet when a prepared SwissQrCodePayload should be rendered into a payment QR image.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a Swiss QR payment code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Renders a Swiss payment QR code from a previously prepared payment payload object.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a Swiss QR code with custom colors and preview ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a branded QR image and opens it immediately after generation.

maml:description.maml:para: Creates a WiFi QR code image.
The generated QR code uses a WiFi payload that can be scanned by mobile devices to prefill network settings.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.
Generates a QR code image containing the provided contact details.
The image format is inferred from the file extension.
Possible values: MeCard, VCard21, VCard3, VCard4
Order of address fields in the QR code.
Contact's title or role within the organization.
Background color of the QR code.
Pixel size for each QR module.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Create a vCard QR code ----------

dev:remarks.maml:para: Creates a simple contact QR code that can be scanned into a phone address book.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Create a richer business contact QR code ----------
command:example.dev:code: New-ImageQRContact -FilePath consultant.png -OutputType VCard4 -Firstname John -Lastname Doe -Email john.doe@evotec.pl -MobilePhone '+48 500 600 700' -Org Evotec -OrgTitle Consultant -Street Example -HouseNumber 15A -City Warsaw -ZipCode '00-001' -Country Poland -Website 'https://evotec.pl'

dev:remarks.maml:para: Generates a business card style QR code with address, organization, and contact metadata.

maml:description.maml:para: Use Degrees for arbitrary angles or for predefined rotations.
Supported formats depend on the file extension.
Possible values: None, Rotate90, Rotate180, Rotate270
Applies a Gaussian sharpen filter with the specified Amount.
Supported formats depend on the file extension.
Specifies the strength of the sharpen filter.
Adds text to an image at the provided coordinates and writes the updated image to disk.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 2: Draw text using ImageSharp ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Adds wrapped text to an image within a box.

command:example.maml:title: ---------- Example 1: Add text inside a box ----------

maml:description.maml:para: Creates thumbnails for all images in a directory.
Possible values: NearestNeighbor, Box, Triangle, Hermite, Lanczos2, Lanczos3, Lanczos5, Lanczos8, MitchellNetravali, CatmullRom, Robidoux, RobidouxSharp, Spline, Welch
Adds a watermark image to another image.
Destination path for the watermarked image.
Watermark placement when coordinates are not specified.
Possible values: TopLeft, TopRight, BottomLeft, BottomRight, Middle
Scale of the watermark relative to the image.
Tile watermark across the image with given spacing.
